Package de.uka.ilkd.key.smt.communication

Class AbstractSolverSocket

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.smt.communication.AbstractSolverSocket
Direct Known Subclasses:
CVC4Socket, CVC5Socket, Z3CESocket, Z3Socket
public abstract class AbstractSolverSocket extends Object
The SolverSocket class describes the communication between the KeY and the SMT solver process. This description is no longer part of the SolverType because in the case when we search for counterexamples and one is found, the model has to be queried. This query depends on the SMT problem and is not the same for all solvers of a given type.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    protected static final int
    FINISH
    Indicates that the solver already sent a sat/unsat/unknown result.
    protected static final int
    WAIT_FOR_DETAILS
    Indicates that the socket waits for more details (a model or a proof).
    protected static final int
    WAIT_FOR_MODEL
    Indicates that the socket waits for a model to be produced by the solver.
    protected static final int
    WAIT_FOR_QUERY
    Indicates that the socket waits for the result to a query (used by CE generator).
    protected static final int
    WAIT_FOR_RESULT
    Indicates that the solver has not yet sent a sat/unsat/unknown result.

  • Field Details

    • WAIT_FOR_RESULT

      protected static final int WAIT_FOR_RESULT
      Indicates that the solver has not yet sent a sat/unsat/unknown result.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • WAIT_FOR_DETAILS

      protected static final int WAIT_FOR_DETAILS
      Indicates that the socket waits for more details (a model or a proof).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • WAIT_FOR_QUERY

      protected static final int WAIT_FOR_QUERY
      Indicates that the socket waits for the result to a query (used by CE generator).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • WAIT_FOR_MODEL

      protected static final int WAIT_FOR_MODEL
      Indicates that the socket waits for a model to be produced by the solver. This is a special version of WAIT_FOR_DETAILS only used by the CE generator.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • FINISH

      protected static final int FINISH
      Indicates that the solver already sent a sat/unsat/unknown result.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractSolverSocket

      protected AbstractSolverSocket(@Nonnull String name, ModelExtractor query)
      Creates a new solver socket with given solver name and ModelExtractor.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the solver in use
      query - the ModelExtractor used to extract a counterexample

  • Method Details

    • getQuery

      public ModelExtractor getQuery()

    • setQuery

      public void setQuery(ModelExtractor query)

    • getName

      protected String getName()

    • messageIncoming

      public abstract void messageIncoming(@Nonnull Pipe pipe, @Nonnull String msg) throws IOException
      Invoked when the solver has sent a new message to its stdout or stderr.
      Parameters:
      pipe - the Pipe that received the message
      msg - the message as String
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs

    • modifyProblem

      public String modifyProblem(String problem)
      Modify an SMT problem String in some way (e.g. prepend some SMT commands). By default, the String is not changed at all.
      Parameters:
      problem - the SMT problem String to be modified
      Returns:
      a modified version of the problem

    • createSocket

      @Nonnull public static AbstractSolverSocket createSocket(@Nonnull SolverType type, ModelExtractor query)
      Creates a new solver socket that can handle the communication for the given solver type.
      Parameters:
      type - the SolverType to create the socket for
      query - the ModelExtractor that can be used to extract a counterexample (for non-CE solvers this can be null)
      Returns:
      the newly created socket

    • copy

      public abstract AbstractSolverSocket copy()
      Returns:
      a shallow copy of the socket at hand (new object with the same class and identical attributes)
